items.title: Trump vows to hit Iran "hard," Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs"
items.standfirst: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as President Trump pledges new strikes on Iran tonight, warning Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.
items.content: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as President Trump pledges new strikes on Iran tonight, warning Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

items.content: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

items.title: Trump vows U.S. will strike Iran again today: "They keep playing us for suckers"
items.standfirst: President Trump said Iran will "pay the price" for taking too long to negotiate a deal, adding that the U.S. will hit Iran "hard." This comes after a U.S. Apache helicopter was downed, sparking an exchange of fire. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has the latest.
items.content: President Trump said Iran will "pay the price" for taking too long to negotiate a deal, adding that the U.S. will hit Iran "hard." This comes after a U.S. Apache helicopter was downed, sparking an exchange of fire. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has the latest.

items.title: Trump says Iran has taken too long to negotiate, will 'pay the price'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. "Iran ‌is all talk and no action," Trump wrote in ⁠a social media post. "They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, ‌now they will have to pay the price!!!"
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. "Iran ‌is all talk and no action," Trump wrote in ⁠a social media post. "They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, ‌now they will have to pay the price!!!"

items.standfirst: Despite calls for calm, masked anti-immigration protesters in Belfast set a bus, vehicles and some homes on fire in what one politician called "a race-based pogrom."
items.content: Despite calls for calm, masked anti-immigration protesters in Belfast set a bus, vehicles and some homes on fire in what one politician called "a race-based pogrom."

items.content: Homes in Belfast were engulfed in flames as unrest continued after police charged a Sudanese man in a knife attack that seriously injured one person.

items.title: Violent anti-immigrant protests flare up across Belfast after knife attack
items.standfirst: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.
items.content: Hundreds of anti-immigrant protesters took to the streets of Belfast on Tuesday, with some setting vehicles alight, after police charged a Sudanese man over a knife attack that left one person with serious neck and head wounds.

Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World

items.title: India summons US envoy over attack on ship carrying Indian sailors off Oman
items.standfirst: Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attack on the Palau-flagged Settebello.
items.content: Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attack on the Palau-flagged Settebello.

items.title: Three Indian crew missing as US says fired on tanker off Oman
items.standfirst: Three Indian crew members were reported missing after a vessel was attacked off Oman, India said Wednesday, before the US military announced it had fired on a tanker attempting to breach its blockade of Iran. In a statement, the Indian foreign ministry condemned the attack on the vessel, the Settebello, saying: "Of the 24 Indian crew onboard, 21 Indians have been rescued thus far and three Indians are reportedly missing". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more.
items.content: Three Indian crew members were reported missing after a vessel was attacked off Oman, India said Wednesday, before the US military announced it had fired on a tanker attempting to breach its blockade of Iran. In a statement, the Indian foreign ministry condemned the attack on the vessel, the Settebello, saying: "Of the 24 Indian crew onboard, 21 Indians have been rescued thus far and three Indians are reportedly missing". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more.

items.title: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman

items.content: India's government said three Indian sailors were missing and 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World, France24

items.title: World's largest whale graveyard found by sub at bottom of ocean
items.standfirst: The researchers saw many strange animals — many believed to be new to science — living off the whale carcasses.
items.content: The researchers saw many strange animals — many believed to be new to science — living off the whale carcasses.

items.title: Whale graveyard dating back five million years discovered
items.standfirst: The Indian Ocean site is "far beyond anything we had imagined", one researcher says.
items.content: The Indian Ocean site is "far beyond anything we had imagined", one researcher says.

items.title: World’s largest, deepest, oldest whale graveyard discovered in Indian Ocean
items.standfirst: Chinese scientists have discovered a massive whale graveyard of around 500 skeletons, some dating back to more than 5 million years, at the bottom of the Indian Ocean west of Australia, according to research published in the journal Nature on Wednesday. The whale fall provides shelter and sustenance to a vibrant ecosystem of marine species, many believed to be new to science.
items.content: Chinese scientists have discovered a massive whale graveyard of around 500 skeletons, some dating back to more than 5 million years, at the bottom of the Indian Ocean west of Australia, according to research published in the journal Nature on Wednesday. The whale fall provides shelter and sustenance to a vibrant ecosystem of marine species, many believed to be new to science.

Vice President JD Vance said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong," but has ultimately "been a good partner" to the U

items.title: Vance says Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong"
items.standfirst: The vice president said President Trump "has been very clear about what is in our best interest" as the relationship with Israel is tested over the Iran war.
items.content: The vice president said President Trump "has been very clear about what is in our best interest" as the relationship with Israel is tested over the Iran war.

items.title: Vance says Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong"
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong," but has ultimately "been a good partner" to the U.S. "We're gonna keep on working together. But where interests diverge, the United States is gonna pursue the best interests of our nation, and that's how it's gonna be," Vance told Robert Costa in an interview airing Sunday.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong," but has ultimately "been a good partner" to the U.S. "We're gonna keep on working together. But where interests diverge, the United States is gonna pursue the best interests of our nation, and that's how it's gonna be," Vance told Robert Costa in an interview airing Sunday.

items.standfirst: US defense secretary continues ramp-up of pressure against country including sanctions and devastating oil blockadePete Hegseth has warned Cuba against acquiring weapons that could threaten the United States, during a visit to the US military base at Guantánamo Bay.Washington has ramped up pressure against Cuba with sanctions and a devastating oil blockade, and Donald Trump has repeatedly signaled that the Cuban government could be the next after Venezuela to fall to US pressure. items.standfirst: Thai woman is in custody in connection with investigation after man was reportedly found dead at a hotel in YangonA US diplomat has been found dead in Myanmar’s largest city, and members of the diplomatic community in Yangon say a Thai woman has been detained by police in connection with the investigation.US officials in Thailand and the US embassy in Myanmar referred questions on the case to the state department, which confirmed the “death of a US government employee” assigned to the embassy in Yangon, but gave no other details.
